entry.title: Parallel Line Branch via SAP for ME
entry.content:
Introduction:

For the users of the 3-system landscape offered by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP for Me not only enables you to provision various tenants but also empowers you to request a branch which copies business configurations from your main line to a new parallel line. For this, you need to follow a few simple steps to create a service request in SAP for Me, and you’ll be able to consume the parallel line. For more details on parallel line, kindly refer to the blog post Introduction to the Parallel Line.

Prerequisites for a Service Request:

Before requesting a branch, it’s imperative to run prerequisite checks to ensure that your main line is ready for the branch, which consists of the following:

  • The Production tenant is provisioned.
  • The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customizing tenant is ready for the branch:
    • All CBC and BC transports in the customizing tenant are released.
  • The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition test tenant is ready for the branch:
    • All transports in the import buffer of the test tenant have been imported.
  • SAP Central Business Configuration is ready for the branch:
    • In the main line workspace all activities and milestones of the “Scope and Organizational Structure” phase are completed.
    • The main line workspace is in the “Product-Specific Configuration” phase.
    • The main line workspace is unlocked.

You can raise a service request by following the below-mentioned steps:

1. Log on to SAP for Me using S-User Administrator credentials.

Fig 1: SAP for Me log-on screen

2. Navigate to the “Service Request” dashboard under the “Private Cloud Workspace”, where you need to click on “Create Service Request”, which will take you to the “Service Catalogue” dashboard.

Fig 2: Service Request dashboard

Fig 3: Service Catalogue dashboard

3. You can search for “Parallel Line: Check Prerequisites”, to display the service.

Fig 4: Parallel Line: Check Prerequisites service

4. When you click on the service, you will get to a new page where you can choose “Add Service”.

Fig 5: Add Service page

5. After choosing “Add Service”, you are redirected to the page where you can select the customizing tenant for which you want to run the prerequisites check.

Fig 6: Adding the prerequisite service

6. A pop-up is displayed where you can select your customizing tenant:

Fig 7: Customizing Tenant pop-up

7. Once you’ve selected the tenant, the “Edit Parameter” button is enabled.

Fig 8: Edit Parameter button enabled

8. After you’ve clicked on the “Edit Parameter” button, you can select the confirmation check box and choose “Check & Set” button.

Fig 9: Confirmation screen

9. Now you can click on the “Submit” button and a new service request will be generated.

Fig 10: Final page

Fig 11: Service Request created

10. When you click on the “Parallel Line: Check Prerequisites” tile, you’ll get to the service information page, where you can switch to the “Communication” tab to see the updated service request.

Fig 12: Communication tab

11. If all the prerequisites are met and there’s no open action item, you will receive a notification email in your inbox and the service request will be updated in SAP for Me.

Fig 13: Successful Handover email

Fig 14: SAP for Me communication

If any of the prerequisites are not met, then the service request will be cancelled and you will be notified via email accordingly:

a. If there are unreleased transport requests of type CBC or BC in the customizing tenant:

Fig 15: Cancelled service request due to open customizing transports

b. If all the transport requests are released from the customizing tenant but not imported into the test tenant:

Fig 16: Cancelled service request due to unimported test transports

c. If the workspace is not in the “Product-Specific Configuration” phase in CBC, that means, not all milestones have been marked as completed:

Fig 17: Cancelled service request due to open milestones

Branch Service Request:

After the prerequisites service request has been completed successfully, you can create a new service request to initiate the branch by following the below-mentioned steps:

1. Steps 1-5 remain the same, but you now need to search for the service catalogue “Parallel Line: Branch”:

Fig 18: Branch service catalogue selection

2. You will then be navigated to the customizing tenant selection, after which you can edit the parameter and click on the “Check & Set” button.

Fig 19: Customizing Tenant selection page

Fig 20: Customizing Tenant pop-up

Fig 21: Confirmation screen

3. You can now see the “Schedule” tile, where you need to select a free execution slot from the search box. You then need to choose OK  and then click on the “Check and Set” button for the selected schedule.

Fig 22: Schedule tile

Fig 23: Schedule selection page

Fig 24: Time slot selection pop-up

Fig 25: Check & Set the Schedule

4. On the final page, you need to click on “Submit”. With this action, a service request will be generated, and you will be navigated to the home screen, where you can check the “Communication” tab for any further responses from the SAP team.

Fig 26: Final page

Fig 27: Branch service request creation

Fig 28: Branch service request details

5. Once the branch is done, you will receive a notification and a handover email with the new system details and the service request will be updated accordingly.

Fig 29: Branch Notification email

Conclusion:

By following these steps, you can seamlessly create a parallel line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using SAP for Me. Ensuring that all prerequisites are met before initiating the branching process is crucial to avoid service request cancellations. This streamlined process enables an efficient system landscape management.

entry.title: Re: How to set rounding for picking only a full HU or bag...
entry.content: I have a similar situation and in food industry. The customer only wants to pull full pallets for picking for staging for production. I have the rounding set on the process type S220 staging for production. What I have found is that the rounding works when there is 1 batch on the pallet on the Handling Unit. If there are two batches for example the PMR is for 500lbs and the and the handling unit has two batches that are 1000lbs each it will round to the the first batch to the 1000lbs but not request the second batch be pulled for the entire HU. Anu thoughts on this? I would for it to pull the 2000lbs but it seems to be batch specific. They dont want this as a bulk storage. The storage type is standard storage allowing HU in the storage type. Any help on this would awesome

entry.title: Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success 2025
entry.content:
NEWS 2025 - Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success

"Digital Adoption" ist in aller Munde – aber was bedeutet das konkret für Unternehmen? Mein Kollege Thomas Jenewein hat in seinem aufschlussreichen Blogbeitrag die verschiedenen Aspekte und betroffenen Bereiche dieses Konzepts anschaulich dargelegt.

Ein Kernaspekt der digitalen Adoption ist die Anpassungsfähigkeit an neue technologische Möglichkeiten. Anders ausgedrückt: Es geht darum, wie Unternehmen neue digitale Werkzeuge und Technologien effektiv nutzen und in ihren Arbeitsalltag integrieren. Diese Herausforderung stellt viele Organisationen vor komplexe Aufgaben – und genau hier setzt SAP Preferred Success an.

Wie genau unterstützt SAP Preferred Success Unternehmen bei ihrer digitalen Transformation?

SAP Preferred Success ist weit mehr als nur ein Supportmodell – es ist ein umfassender Begleiter für Unternehmen auf ihrer Cloud-Reise mit SAP-Lösungen. Der Service bietet nicht nur Unterstützung bei der initialen Implementierung, sondern steigert langfristig die Produktivität und Effizienz von Organisationen in der Cloud.

Im Mittelpunkt steht ein dedizierter Customer Success Manager, der in regelmäßigen Abstimmungsmeetings einen maßgeschneiderten Erfolgsplan erstellt und dessen Umsetzung begleitet.

Dieser Plan bedient sich aus einem großen Katalog verschiedener Services, die auf die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des jeweiligen Unternehmens zugeschnitten werden. Dazu gehören Solution Launch Checks und Success Checks, die eine optimale Konfiguration der SAP-Lösungen sicherstellen und Verbesserungspotenziale aufdecken.

Ein Highlight ist die detaillierte Release-Begleitung, die tiefe Einblicke in neue Software-Versionen und deren Auswirkungen bietet. Dies ermöglicht eine proaktive Planung und erleichtert die Anpassung an neue Funktionalitäten.

Zur Förderung der kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung beinhaltet das Programm fünf Lizenzen für den SAP Learning Hub. Diese ermöglichen den Zugang zu gezielten Schulungsprogrammen, die auf die spezifischen Anforderungen des Unternehmens abgestimmt sind.

SAP Preferred Success bietet zudem erweiterten Support mit verbesserten Service Level Agreements. Dies gewährleistet schnellere Reaktionszeiten und effizientere Problemlösungen. Bei komplexen Herausforderungen stehen dem Unternehmen Fachexperten mit fundiertem Know-how zur Seite.

Insgesamt zielt SAP Preferred Success darauf ab, Unternehmen die notwendigen Werkzeuge, das erforderliche Wissen und die benötigte Unterstützung zu bieten, um ihre digitale Transformation erfolgreich zu gestalten und den maximalen Nutzen aus ihren SAP-Lösungen zu ziehen.

Eine unabhängige Studie von Forrester Consulting hat die Vorteile von SAP Preferred Success untersucht und quantifiziert. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass Unternehmen, die das Programm nutzen, erhebliche Verbesserungen erzielen konnten. Dazu gehören eine gesteigerte Produktivität, reduzierte Systemausfallzeiten und beschleunigte Einführung und Updates. Die Erkenntnisse aus der Studie unterstreichen den Wertbeitrag von SAP Preferred Success für Unternehmen, die ihre SAP-Investitionen optimal nutzen möchten.

Kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung: Neuerungen bei SAP Preferred Success

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um mit den sich wandelnden Anforderungen der Kunden und den Fortschritten in der SAP-Technologie Schritt zu halten. In den vergangenen Jahren wurde das Programm nicht nur für eine breitere Palette von SAP-Lösungen ausgerollt, sondern auch um innovative Services erweitert.

Eine Übersicht der SAP-Lösungen, für die SAP Preferred Success verfügbar ist, einschließlich detaillierter Informationen zu jeder Lösung, findet sich hier.

SAP hat mit der Einführung der "Expanded Edition" von Preferred Success einen bedeutenden Schritt nach vorn gemacht. Diese erweiterte Version richtet sich an Unternehmen, die vor komplexeren Herausforderungen stehen oder eine intensivere Betreuung bei der Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen wünschen.

Zusammengefasst bieten die Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success eine Reihe von Vorteilen:

  • Tiefgreifenderes Expertenwissen durch einen erweiterten Zugang zu Fachleuten, die ihr umfangreiches SAP Know-How einbringen.
  • Zusätzliche Unterstützung: Neben dem Customer Success Manager steht ein Produktspezialist zur Verfügung, der detaillierte Einblicke bietet.
  • Verbesserte Supportleistungen, um noch schneller und effektiver auf Kundenanfragen reagieren zu können.

Auch zu den Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success gibt es eine unabhängige Forrester-Studie, die deren Vorteile für Unternehmen analysiert und bewertet hat. Laut dieser Studie erzielten Unternehmen eine signifikante Effizienzsteigerung. Dies äußerte sich vor allem in einem schnelleren Zugang zu SAP-Expertise, einer deutlichen Reduzierung externer Partnerkosten sowie erheblichen Zeitersparnissen bei der Verwaltung von Anpassungen. Darüber hinaus stellte die Studie eine Verbesserung des internen Fachwissens, eine Beschleunigung von Innovationsprozessen und eine Minderung von Systemrisiken fest.

Neben der Einführung von SAP Preferred Success für zusätzliche SAP-Lösungen und der Erweiterung durch die Expanded Edition für einige der Lösungen, hat SAP Preferred Success auch sein Serviceportfolio erweitert, um den sich wandelnden Bedürfnissen der Kunden gerecht zu werden. Dabei ist zu beachten, dass sich die angebotenen Services je nach SAP-Lösung unterscheiden, um eine möglichst passgenaue Unterstützung zu gewährleisten.

Ein besonderer Schwerpunkt liegt auf den neu eingeführten AI Accelerator Services. Diese innovativen Dienste sind darauf ausgerichtet, Unternehmen bei der Nutzung künstlicher Intelligenz in ihren SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen und sie auf dem Weg zu einer KI-gestützen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmensführung zu begleiten. Obwohl die spezifischen Funktionen je nach SAP-Produkt variieren, verfolgen die AI Accelerator Services generell drei Hauptziele:

  1. Innovation: Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung und Implementierung neuer, KI-gestützter Geschäftsmodelle und Prozesse.
  2. Automatisierung: Hilfe bei der Identifizierung und Umsetzung von Automatisierungsmöglichkeiten durch KI, um Effizienz und Produktivität zu steigern.
  3. Prozessoptimierung: Beratung zur Verbesserung bestehender Geschäftsabläufe durch den Einsatz von KI-Technologien.

Beispielsweise könnten die AI Accelerator Services für SAP S/4HANA Kunden dabei unterstützen, intelligente Vorhersagemodelle für die Bestandsoptimierung zu implementieren. Für SAP SuccessFactors-Nutzer könnten sie Hilfestellung bei der Einführung KI-gestützter Rekrutierungsprozesse bieten.

Eine weitere Sparte neuer SAP Preferred Success Services kommt aus dem Bereich Organizational Change Management (OCM). Um dieses Thema abzudecken, das für die Digital Adoption aus menschlicher Sicht unverzichtbar ist, hat das Digital Change and Adoption Team der SAP zwei Services ausgearbeitet:

  • den Transformation Mindset Insights Service sowie
  • den Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service.

Beide Services nähern sich dem Thema Transformation aus der menschlichen Perspektive, denn der Fakt bleibt: Ohne die Mitarbeiter*innen kann keine Transformation erfolgreich werden.

Der Transformation Mindset Insights Service von SAP hilft Organisationen, ihren Reifegrad in Bezug auf Transformationsfähigkeit zu verstehen und kontinuierlich zu verbessern.

Der Prozess umfasst:

  • Analyse der sechs Mindset Traits, namentlich Resilienz, Systemisches Denken, Kontinuierliche Entwicklung, Cross-Kollaboration, Offenheit für Veränderung und Situative Adaptivität.
  • Datenbasierter Ansatz mit Management-Sicht und Mitarbeiterbefragungen.
  • Workshops zur Erarbeitung einer Roadmap zur Verbesserung des Transformation Mindset Reifegrads.
  • Umsetzung von Maßnahmen in einer mit dem Kunden vereinbarten Aktionsphase.
  • Erneute Erhebung des Reifegrads zur Überprüfung der Effektivität der Maßnahmen.
  • Kontinuierliche Arbeit am Transformation Mindset

So können Organisationen kontinuierlich an ihrem Transformation Mindset arbeiten und das Expertenwissen der SAP bei Bedarf zur Unterstützung heranziehen, um so den neuen Herausforderungen unserer stetig wandelnden Welt einen Schritt vorauszubleiben.

Ein zufriedener Kunde hatte Folgendes zu den Ergebnissen des Transformation Mindset Insights Service zu sagen: „[Der Service] zeigt genau, was wir im letzten Jahr vermutet hatten. Er fängt die Gefühle ein, die mir die Leute hinter verschlossenen Türen mitgeteilt haben. Diese kann ich dem Management nun anhand realer Daten zeigen. Die Ergebnisse spiegeln unsere Situation sehr gut wider.“

Der Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service richtet sich derweil hauptsächlich an Kunden der Human Capital Management (HCM) Sparte der SAP Lösungen, welche den Reifegrad ihrer OCM Aktivitäten verstehen und verbessern möchten, bevor sie ihre Transformation beginnen.
SAPs OCM Expert*innen evaluieren in einem datenbasierten Vorgehen bestehende OCM Aktivitäten und Konzepte der Kunden und erarbeiten basierend auf den Ergebnissen gemeinsam mit den Kunden Verbesserungsempfehlungen für ihre OCM- und Trainingsplanung. So können Organisationen auch ohne ein ganzes OCM Engagement sicherstellen, dass sie einen gut informierten und effektiven OCM Ansatz für ihre Transformation verfolgen.

Kunden können sich für detaillierte Informationen zu den verfügbaren Services für ihre spezifischen SAP-Lösungen an ihren Customer Success Manager wenden.

Fazit:

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um den Herausforderungen der digitalen Transformation gerecht zu werden. Mit der Expanded Edition und neuen Services wie den AI Accelerator Services oder Change Management Services unterstreicht SAP sein Ziel, Kunden bei der optimalen Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen.

Dabei wird individuell auf Kundenbedürfnisse eingegangen und die Wertschöpfung aus SAP-Investitionen verbessert. So bleiben Unternehmen für zukünftige Anforderungen gut gerüstet.

Informieren Sie sich auf SAP Preferred Success, werfen Sie einen Blick auf die ausgesuchten Informationen unten, wenden Sie sich an Ihre SAP-Ansprechperson oder nehmen Sie mit uns Kontakt auf.

 

Links auf weitere Blogbreiträge und Artikel zu SAP Preferred Success:

Auf Erfolgskurs mit SAP Preferred Success: Einblicke in den Alltag eines Customer Success Partners

Kundenreferenz mit Lufthansa Technik: “Back to standard” – Wie SAP Preferred Success Lufthansa bei der digitalen Adoption unterstützt

SAP Preferred Success: Transforming Challenges into Opportunities for Companies Worldwide in 2024

Aktuelle Kundenreferenzen in MEE:

SAP Preferred Success for S/4HANA

SAP Preferred Success for HCM

SAP Preferred Success für Digital Supply Chain

entry.title: How to get information about Sales Document Item Texts by Text Types？
entry.content:
Hi there,

We need to get information about Sales Document Item Texts by text types in Embedded SAC. We did not find any suitable standard CDS views.

How we can get this information?

Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get information about Sales Document Item Text...
entry.content:
Hello!

The storage mechanism of Text is special. It is stored by binary characters. For this reason, it cannot be directly displayed to end users through CDS views.

Instead of CDS View, you can consider using the OData V4 API Sales Order (A2X), with GET method and "/SalesOrderText" and "filter".

For example: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_salesorder/srvd_a2x/sap/SalesOrderText?$top=20

You can add filter of LongTextID.

Check below for more details about this API:

  • Business Accelerator Hub - Sales Order (A2X)
  • Help Portal - Sales Order (A2X, OData V4)

For any further questions, you can feel free to reply under this SAP Community Q&A.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: DVM report shows less memory for archiving and large number of "not analyzed" objects
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support,


We have activated DVM for our managed system and generated reports for Top 30 objects, Top 30 growing table pre week and Tables showing hights data based activity. Report gives only 8.35 GB for Reduction Potential out of 425,26 GB.
Report shows  327,52 GB not analyzed. How we can anlyze those area also. Please suggest how we can to achieve more Reduction Potential information.



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: DVM report shows less memory for archiving and large...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

I have thoroughly analyzed the "Not Analyzed" objects section in the attached DVM report as per our discussion. Here are the key findings from the analysis: 
- The five tables you specified for analysis have a combined size of 20,35 GB.
- The total size of the DB is 425,26 GB.
- Therefore, the size of the analyzed tables is approximately 15% of the total data size.

This proportion seems reasonable. The reason for the low portion of analyzed tables could be that the tables specified are not the largest in the system.

Here are my recommendations:
- An analysis of the top 30 tables could increase the portion of analyzed tables.
- It's important to note that the objects not analyzed does not imply that these objects are not growing. They were simply not selected for analysis.

For further assistance, you may find the following KBAs helpful:

2757079 - How to create DVM report for specific objects https://me.sap.com/notes/2757079.

2569069 - Explanation of Chapter “Analyzed Objects” in DVM Service reports https://me.sap.com/notes/2569069

entry.title: Re: How to make input field accept only numeric values in...
entry.content:
You can prevent an Input from accepting chars that are not digits by adding an event delegate on keypress:

oInput.addEventDelegate({
  onkeypress: function(oEvent) {
    if (oEvent.key < '0' || oEvent.key > '9') {
      oEvent.preventDefault()
    }
  }
})

entry.title: Possibilities and Limitations of GROW with SAP - Core HR Add-On
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am exploring the GROW with SAP – Core HR Add-On and would like to understand its full capabilities and constraints.

Since there is very little documentation or resources available on this subject, we are facing immediate challenges in implementation. Specially because client has purchased the GROW with SAP - Core HR Add-On license but has not opted for the SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) license. We are trying to understand the full scope of what this add-on offers and its limitations compared to a full SuccessFactors EC implementation.

Could the SAP community help clarify:

Core Functionalities: What HR functionalities are available with the Core HR Add-On?

Limitations: What key features from SuccessFactors EC are not included in the Core HR Add-On?

Integration Possibilities: How does it integrate with other SAP modules (like Payroll, Time Tracking, or S/4HANA)?

Customization and Extensibility: Can we configure or extend this solution to meet specific client needs?

Implementation Considerations: Are there any best practices or challenges to keep in mind when deploying this add-on?

Since this is a new area with limited insights, any real-world experience, documentation, or reference materials would be highly valuable to us and the broader SAP community.

 

Warm Regards,

Aratrika Bardhan

entry.title: Re: How to make input field accept only numeric values in sapui5 using XML?
entry.content:
This solution doesn't prevent an input from accepting non-digits - the character is accepted and then later removed.

For example if you've got the value bound to a model and there's an event registered on the binding or on the model then they will get fired twice.

A better solution is to use an event delegate on keypress.

entry.title: In IAS, if user is non-admin then he is not able to see the Privacy Policy document or Terms of use
entry.content:
In IAS, once a user (non-admin - usertype='employee') accept the consent, he is not able view it in IAS console., If IAS doesn't allow non-admin to view it in IAS console then is there a way(URL/api) thru which BTP application can pull this content from IAS and show it?

if we still want to let tenant users to revoke the accepted consent the only option with IAS is to load a new version so that, all users start seeing it and either accept/deny. It is sounding complex!!! 

SAP IAS is handling authentication and consent management today, but it does not expose APIs for users to view or revoke consent, switching to SAP Enterprise Consent and Preference Management (ECPM) means we will need to manually enforce consent collection within your BTP application.

what is the overall guideline?

entry.title: Re: Automatic rounding difference posting to GL account i...
entry.content:
Hello.

Here the solution for this issue. Just proceed to set as per below:

 

In SAP, when posting vendor invoices using transaction code FB60, rounding differences can occur due to discrepancies between the invoice amount and the system’s calculation, especially when dealing with foreign currencies or amounts that don’t align with the system’s rounding rules. To manage these differences effectively, you can configure the system to handle rounding discrepancies appropriately.

Steps to Configure Rounding Differences in SAP:

  1. Define Accounts for Rounding Differences:
    • Use transaction code OB00 to specify which General Ledger (G/L) accounts should be used to post rounding differences. This ensures that any minor discrepancies are automatically posted to designated accounts, maintaining financial accuracy. 
    • Navigate to SPRO → SAP Reference IMG → Financial Accounting → Accounts Receivable and Accounts Payable → Business Transactions → Outgoing Payments → Outgoing Payments Global Settings → Define Accounts for Rounding Differences. 
    • In this configuration, assign the appropriate G/L account for rounding differences. This account will be used to post any minor differences that arise during invoice processing. 
  2. Set Tolerance Limits:
    • Establish tolerance limits to control the maximum allowable rounding differences. This helps prevent posting insignificant amounts that don’t materially affect financial reporting. 
    • Navigate to SPRO → SAP Reference IMG → Materials Management → Logistics Invoice Verification → Invoice Block → Set Tolerance Limits. 
    • Define the tolerance limits for your company code, specifying the maximum permissible rounding differences. 
  3. Utilize Complex Posting for Invoice Verification:
    • When posting invoices that may result in rounding differences, consider using the ‘Complex Posting’ feature to manually adjust and allocate rounding amounts. 
    • In the ‘Create Incoming Invoices’ app, navigate to Menu → Environment → Complex Posting. Here, you can enter the G/L account and the rounding off amount manually to ensure accurate posting.

 

Additional Considerations:

 

  • Ensure that the rounding rules and decimal settings align with your organization’s financial policies and the currencies in use.
  • Regularly monitor and reconcile the accounts used for posting rounding differences to maintain financial integrity. 

 

 

By implementing these configurations, SAP will automatically handle minor rounding discrepancies during invoice processing, ensuring that such differences are posted to appropriate G/L accounts without manual intervention. This approach streamlines financial operations and maintains accurate financial records.

entry.title: Re: IFRS 15 - Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Hi Sanne,

Thank you for your question. Let me provide you with the link to the SAP Note regarding the activation of IFRS15 again: 2524569 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Information of IFRS15/ASC 606 Revenue Recognition Functionality - SAP for Me

Alternatively, you can also find information about IFRS15 and revenue recognition on the SAP Help Portal: General Overview of Revenue Recognition for Multiple-Element Arrangements | SAP Help Portal

However, if you want to achieve IFRS15 compliance with Event-Based Revenue Recognition, we recommend that you take a closer look at Universal Revenue Recognition | SAP Help Portal, as it allows for adherence to standards provided by IFRS15.

I hope that helps.

 Best regards,

Janina

The API API_CLFN_CHARACTERISTIC is used for updating characteristics data the same as using the Manage Characteristics.

 

An API to update object classification for the likes of a DIR is not available

entry.title: Where can I find learning materials on how to apply the machine learning workflow?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Developing Classification Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you will explore how to apply the machine learning workflow. The steps from feature extraction to model evaluation are illustrated to ensure a comprehensive understanding of the process. The scenario presented in this learning journey focuses on preventing employee churn, a key business problem addressed using SAP HANA Cloud Machine Learning.

Learning objectives
By the end of this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Configure the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA (hana-ml) and connect it to SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Access and visually explore SAP HANA data using HANA DataFrames in Python.
  • Train a classification model for predicting employee churn rate using hana-ml Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) functions.
  • Perform model evaluation to assess the accuracy and effectiveness of the trained model.

Prerequisites

If you need some foundational information, you can also refer to
Developing AI Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Automate Job Requisition Updates from Position Data Using Integration Center
entry.content: Consultants, if you're encountering issues with job failures caused by mandatory fields, here's a solution: You can set the isdraft=true value for IC jobs. To implement this, create a hidden field in the Position object of boolean type, ensure its value is always set to true, and then map this hidden field to the "isdraft" field. This approach should resolve the problem effectively.

entry.title: AI-based Analytics: SAP Analytics Cloud 'Just Ask' on top of SAP Digital Manufacturing data
entry.content:
Introduction

Learn how SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Digital Manufacturing advanced analytics and AI-driven insights can give you fast information access and insights, enabling data-driven decisions with confidence.

Whether you're checking historical Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) across plants, tracking production orders, or analyzing downtime metrics, this scenario showcases how advanced analytics can transform operations for peak efficiency and agility.

Using intuitive natural language queries with SAC's 'Just Ask' to comprehensive As-Built and Where-Used reports, this solution simplifies data exploration like never before. Gain instant insights by asking simple questions, create personalized dashboards effortlessly, and ensure full transparency across manufacturing processes.

See how SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Digital Manufacturing can drive productivity, innovation, and next-level operational excellence in your organization.

Technical Integration

To begin with, OData Services Connection from SAP Analytics Cloud to SAP Digital Manufacturing OData V4 API for scheduled data import must be configured.

For more details, see:

  • SAP Digital Manufacturing for insights APIs
  • Manufacturing Data Objects
  • 3463893 - Additional information on Digital Manufacturing for insights APIs (New)

Analytical Dashboards

Once data is available, complex analytical dashboards and stories can be created, including great SAC capabilities like forecast and predictions.

Just Ask

Just Ask enables you to use natural language to make queries on top of the manufacturing data and gain unique insights. See some examples below:

As-Built Report

Using 'Just Ask', even more complex reports like 'As-Built' can be achieved very simply.

Where-Used

Using 'Just Ask', even more complex reports like 'Where-Used' for full end-to-end traceability can be achieved very simply.

Creating personalized Dashboards, based on 'Just Ask' insights

Finally, you can create personalized dashboards effortlessly, on top of 'Just Ask' insights.

Anytime in 'Just Ask' results, you can click ‘Copy’, to copy specific objects.

Which you can then paste in a new story to create your own personalized dashboards, based on 'Just Ask' insights.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: CDS view for planned delivery time in purchasing sche...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

I understand you are seeking assistance with CDS views. Here's what you can do:

1. Refer to KBA :3558790. https://me.sap.com/notes/3558790 This should help you find the relevant CDS view.
2. If there are no available CDS views for the fields you are looking for, you have the option to create an influence request.
3. If no desired BAdI or CDS view is found, this would be considered a new requirement. In this case, I recommend you to post on the customer influence forum.
4. You can also check the API : I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01.

 If you have any other questions or need further clarification, feel free to reach out.

Best regards,
Aishwarya

entry.title: How to dynamically set the basic authentication for the target in a SAP APIM policy?
entry.content:
Hi all,

in this Question Solved: Dynamically set the basic authentication for the t... - SAP Community was the answer, that it is possible to set the BasicAuth-Credentials dynamically via lookup in a KeyValueMap. So I tried it, but I'm not sure if there is only a syntax error or if it's even possible in that way? 

I tried the approach to read a custom attribute of the product and use it in a KeyValueMapOperations-Policy to get the Username and Password Values.

It was no problem to read the custom attributes and save it to a variable. They were visible in Debugging. But I couldn't use them in the following policy:

First try was to set the mapIdentifier dynamically, but I'm not the syntax. Tried it also without { } and to address the variable directly "verifyapikey.CheckAPIKey.apiproduct.CredentialAlias".

No success 😞 the Authorization Header was always created with empty Username and Password.
Basic Og== which means "Basic :"

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="{dynamicCredentialAlias}" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
	<Get assignTo="private.auth.Username">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter>Username</Parameter>
	    </Key>
	</Get>
	<Get assignTo="private.auth.Password">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter>Password</Parameter>
	    </Key>
	</Get>	
	<Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

 The second approach was to set the <Parameter>-Tags dynamically, but it leads to the same result as the first approach.

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="BasicAuth" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
	<Get assignTo="Username">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter>{dynamicUsername}</Parameter>
	    </Key>
	</Get>
	<Get assignTo="Password">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter>{dynamicPassword}</Parameter>
	    </Key>
	</Get>
	<Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>
<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="BasicAuth" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
	<Get assignTo="private.auth.Username">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter ref="verifyapikey.CheckAPIKey.apiproduct.Username"/>
	    </Key>
	</Get>
	<Get assignTo="private.auth.Password">
	    <Key>
	        <Parameter ref="verifyapikey.CheckAPIKey.apiproduct.Password"/>
	    </Key>
	</Get>
	<Scope>environment</Scope>
</KeyValueMapOperations>

 

Does anyone know how to achieve this dynamic lookup with an encrypted KeyValueMap? I don't want to save the credentials in a plain text custom attribute 😄

Thanks in advance,

Frank

entry.title: Document Information Extraction – Q1 2025 Highlights
entry.content:
Find out what’s new in the Document Information Extraction service.

 

New Excel, Email and Text File Formats

Document Information Extraction, premium edition now supports new Excel, email, and text file types, such as CSV, XLSX, XLSM, DOCX, EMLX, MSG, PAGES, RTF, and TXT. See the complete list sorted by document types in Supported File Types (Premium Edition).

 

New Invoice Header Fields

You can now extract a more comprehensive list of address-related header fields from invoice documents. The list includes Buyer City, Supplier City, Buyer Country Code, and Supplier Country Code. See all of the standard header fields supported by Document Information Extraction in Extracted Header Fields.

 

Decommissioning and Deprecation

As announced in May 2024, the Enrichment Data API Endpoint: Delete Enrichment Data (Synchronous) (DELETE /data) has been decommissioned and is no longer available. Please use the endpoint Delete Enrichment Data (Asynchronous) (DELETE /data/jobs) to delete data records. For more information, check out Delete Enrichment Data (Synchronous) - Decommissioned | SAP Help Portal and Delete Enrichment Data (Asynchronous) | SAP Help Portal.

The Document API endpoint Get Request Payload (GET /document/jobs/{id}/request) is now deprecated and is scheduled for decommissioning in September 2025. Following that date, the endpoint will no longer be available. Find more details in the blog post: Document Information Extraction – Deprecation of D... - SAP Community.

 

Support for the Identity Authentication Service

As of January 2025, you can subscribe to the Document Information Extraction UI using the Identity Authentication service to handle authentication and authorization tasks in SAP BTP. Find all the details in Subscribing to the Document Information Extraction UI With the Identity Authentication Service.

 

Instant Learning  

If you’ve activated instant learning in Document Information Extraction, premium edition, the service can now use your feedback to select the best model for individual fields in SAP schemas for the standard document types: invoice, payment advice, and purchase order. For more information, see Instant Learning (Premium Edition) and Instant Learning (Premium Edition): Best Practices.

 

Export and Import Feature for Instant Learning

You can now export and import documents that you've edited and confirmed for use with instant learning. This new feature of the Document Information Extraction UI allows you to quickly and easily export documents along with all information relevant for instant learning and import them for use in another context. See Export/Import Documents. 

You can also export documents and associated information, such as ground truth and related instant learning data, via the new Document API endpoint Export Document Details.

 

More Meaningful Extraction Confidence Ranges

Document Information Extraction, premium edition now provides more meaningful extraction confidence ranges for fields with the setup type auto and no default extractor – before you enter any feedback on results. The service does so by checking whether the data type of the extracted information matches the data type for the respective field in the schema.

 

Stay up to date with all the latest developments for Document Information Extraction – by following the tag #DocumentInformationExtraction.

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
Mindset

A highly insightful and engaging read for professionals, students, teachers, and everyone alike. Well worth the time! The first chapter alone is enough to set the stage 😊

"mindset" by Carol S. Dweck explores how adopting a growth mindset - the belief that abilities and intelligence can be developed through effort and learning - leads to greater success, resilience, and personal growth compared to a fixed mindset, which sees abilities as innate and unchangeable.

Mindset

entry.title: Re: MDG. How to turn off auto replication of materials
entry.content: Oleg_Turlov: Replication is supposed to be triggered at the last step. If you keep the replication step in BRF+ then multiple replication is bound to trigger. You then need to explicitly control that via enhancement.

entry.title: Re: Cost planning in Cj40 is not availble in Cj30 to dist...
entry.content:
Hi Adam!

When Level 1 WBS element is grayed in CJ30 (see WBS elements ST-0138-C and ST-0138-L), it means that the budget is not being entered directly in t-code CJ30. Instead, the budget comes from an investment position (IM module) via t-code IM52.

The WBS element ST-0138-C has received a budget and can therefore distribute it in CJ30 to the lower-level WBS elements ST-0138-C-10, ST-0138-C-20, and ST-0138-C-99. In this case, 100% of the budget was allocated to WBS element ST-0138-C-20. On the other hand, WBS element ST-0138-L has not received any budget.

In CJ30, you can only distribute the budget from the Level 1 WBS element to its lower levels. To assign the investment program budget to the project, you must use t-code IM52.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: Working with the new My Timesheet App Version 4 (F3074A) and tasks time-reporting in PPM Projects
entry.content:
The new Fiori App "My Timesheet" offers a comprehensive alternative to the popular CAT2 transaction. In this Blog Entry I will show how the new APP, My Timesheet (S/4HANA Version 4) Overview, can be used in an integrative way to report time related to Tasks in PPM Projects.

Before we dive into the use case, I will present some highlights of the new App.

 

 

Upon opening the App we land in the main “Time Sheet” screen. Here one is presented with the time recordings for the week, if none were made for this week (like in the screenshot above), (1) three links on the bottom of the page are available that enable the user to either add a time entry, make a quick entry, or simply copy the entries from last week. The user can freely switch between two views (2) a vertical one, which is the default one, and a horizontal one which mimics the style of the CAT2 transaction. Unlike the transaction CAT2, where the user has to choose a profile before proceeding to a different page for recording their time, in the new app (3) the user is able to switch seemingly between all profiles as deemed necessary.

Multiple ways of providing a time entry can be put in use. By clicking on the Edit button (4) or, in the case where no entry has been made for the actual week, one can simply click on Add time Entry. From there the user has to choose one or more assignment for time entry. Another option is to have an administrator assign assignments to users and transfer them to the user with the help of a report. This method is further explained in SAP Note 3444277.

 

There are 4 manual ways in using a time attribute in order to record time:

  • Assignment and Assignment Group: Users can create favorites in the form of assignments and assignment groups during time recording. Assignment info (Attribute hyperlink provided with assignment name) will display details of the time entry. A Group is simply a collection of single Assignments. The assignments are created either by the user themselves or by an admin that allocates assignments to the respective user.
  • Worklist: depending on the Profile settings, a list of automatically generated Attributes are presented. These Attributes can represent for example Tasks in PPM that are assigned to the user.
  • Form Entry: a new time attribute can be generated on-the-spot in relation to attributes such as “Activity Type”, “Receiver WBS Element”, and “Sender Cost Center”. This method can be useful for ad-hoc time entries where no assignments are available or accessible, or for a one-off time entry.

 

The last highlight goes deeper and into Customizing. Under the following path:

  • Personnel Management

--> Roles and Self-Services (SAP Fiori)

--> Employee Self-Service (SAP Fiori)

--> Application-Specific Settings

--> My Timesheet

--> Enable or Disable Features

This highlight offers the functionality for admins to switch features on or off according to their requirements:

 

 

Settings for My Timesheet (Version 4) and Pre-requisites for PPM

 

As described in the online help of My Timesheet (Version 4) app, there are some prerequisite configurations required:

Data Entry Profile Creation:

In transaction CAC1, create a data entry profile. Select configurations in the profile as per requirement.

An important setting to pay attention to that relates to PPM Projects in the Data Entry Profiles in order to show Assignments from Projects to the user in the Worklist is checking the following box to enable the automatic transfer of entries into the Worklist:

 

 

Profile Fields Configuration:

In transaction CAC2, configure the Influencing Data Entry section for the profile you have created.

 

CVR Parameter Setup:

In transaction SU01, maintain CVR Parameter as the default Data Entry Profile that is to be used while recording time for your user.

 

Assign Personnel Number to User:

In transaction PA30, assign a personnel number to your SAP user by maintaining Communication Infotype value as IT0105 and Subtype as 0001.

 

Maintain Planned Working Time:

In transaction PA30, maintain the Planned Working Time for the personnel number in IT0007.

 

Assign the BP role BUP003 Employee to the users:

In transaction BP, ensure that the role BUP003 is maintained. This ensures the creation of a Business Partner (1) that can be used in PPM as a resource. This Business Partner is also an Employee (2) and an SAP User (3).

 

 

 

Time Recording for Projects in Portfolio and Project Management

Now to the use-case, we start by creating a Project in Project Management (PPM) and by choosing the right Project type that enables the automatic creation of a Project Systems (PS) Project with WBS Elements for the transfer of time recordings from the Timesheet App into PS and thus subsequently into PPM. The Project type settings must be set up in Customizing for Accounting Integration.

Once the PPM Project is created and Tasks are staffed, we release the Project and flag it for transfer. In our example the PS Project is created automatically along with the corresponding WBS Elements representing the tasks in the PPM Project. Time recording is supported for role- or task-based time recording and the PS Project assignment is created either manually or automatically in the accounting integration scenarios. In this use-case the option of automatic creation of PS Project (and thus WBS elements) according to PPM task-based time recording is used.

The Project is now created and flagged for transfer. Under the Accounting Tab in the PPM Project one can check for the automatic assignment of the Tasks with the corresponding WBS Elements that are now created along with a PS project automatically with a one-to-one relationship:

 

 

We proceed now to the Fiori App, My Timesheet (F3074A), in order to start with the time-recording activity. First, we switch our Profile to the preferred one, the one with Project assignments (see settings section above), after that, in the “Time Sheet” tab, we start our time entry by clicking on "Add Time Entry".

 

 

 

Since we have set up our Profile to show Project assignments, we will start by looking for our assignments through the Worklist that is automatically generated. To do so we open the Select Assignment search option by clicking on the double squares:

A window will pop-up and from there we switch to the "Worklist" Tab:

 

In the Worklist, all assignments allocated to the user are shown in the list. The name of the PPM Task is displayed, in this case the two tasks Material Procurement, Material Delivery, as well as the Receiver WBS elements respectively:

 

 

 

We choose the relevant assignment from the suggestions by clicking on it. A new window will open automatically, and the assignment (1) is directly copied to the “Time Sheet”.

By clicking on “Attributes” one can check the details/description of this Assignment (Task ID and WBS Element) (2), however, to permanently show the details of the Assignment as a text under each entry, the BADI: HRFIO_B_TSH_FUNCTIONAL with the Method: HRFIO_IF_TSH_FUNCTIONAL~GET_ATTRIBUTES_LIST can be used. This enhancement displays the description of the Attribute for each entry permanently below each of the Assignment Entry Fields.

We will proceed now by switching the View to the Horizontal Time Entry View (3).

 

This view might be a preferred one among users familiar with the CAT2 transaction. This view provides a weekly overview (1) to the entered Assignments and enables the direct entry of values throughout the whole week. From here one can simply add multiple assignments for the week and accordingly record the required time (2):

 

This might enable a better overview for the user and ensure that our required target hours (set in TA:PA30) are not exceeded.

In the case where the target hours are exceeded, a warning will pop-up to inform the user:

 

 I will now enter the time spent on this Task, a total of 11 hours, as follows:

 

After submitting the Entries, we want to transfer them into the Project Task (PPM). In order to do that we need to run the report DPR_CATS_CPR_TRANSF. This report can also be set up to run automatically. In this case we will run it manually:

 

Upon running the report, the hours recorded are transferred into the Task and made visible. Once all hours are recorded the Task can be closed/confirmed in the PPM Project. The confirmed time-recordings can also be transferred as actual costs in CO.

 

In order to display actual costs, we use the transaction: CJI3N. Other transactions are also available. 

 

As a conclusion, the updated Fiori App "My Timesheet" (Version 4) provides an alternative to the traditional CAT2 transaction for time reporting in PPM Projects. It offers a user-friendly interface with flexible views and multiple methods for time entry, making it easier for users to manage and record their time. Key configurations and settings ensure seamless integration with PPM Projects, allowing for automatic creation of project elements and efficient transfer of recorded hours. This enhances overall project management and cost tracking, streamlining the time-reporting process.

 

For additional resources regarding Configuration, setting up the app, or more information on the topic please refer to the following links:

  • Link to the Fiori app Library
  • Link to App Implementation and Configuration (SAP Help portal)
  • Link to Data Entry Profile Settings Blog Entry
  • Link to Information on Assignment and Assignment Group (SAP Note)
  • Link to supported features in the data entry profile (SAP Note)
  • Link to Relevant configuration in My Timesheet V4 (SAP Note)

entry.title: How I Fixed the “GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR” in SAP Migrate Your Data Fiori App
entry.content:
If you’ve been working with SAP’s Migrate Your Data (MYD) Fiori app, you know it’s a powerful tool for transferring data into your S/4HANA system. However, sometimes, even the most straightforward processes can throw you an unexpected curveball.

That’s exactly what happened to us while using the Migrate Your Data app. After uploading data via an XML template, the Simulation process completed successfully, but when we tried to execute the Migrate step, we ran into a roadblock with several error messages.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the exact error we encountered, how I troubleshooted it, and how I ultimately fixed the issue by addressing a misconfiguration in the System Landscape Directory (SLD).

Hopefully, this will save you some time if you encounter the same issue.

The Error: What Happened?

The issue occurred after we followed the usual steps of uploading the XML file (which we downloaded from the Fiori app as a template) and populating it with the necessary data. Everything seemed fine during the Simulation process. No errors there! However, when we hit Migrate, the following errors popped up:

  1. System error occurs when calling function module in target system
  2. System error (portion 0000000001, conversion object Z_CUSTOMER_2_NDV, at 122705)
  3. GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR: An error occurred when determining the business system (conversion object Z_CUSTOMER_2_NDV)

Figure 1. Error messages

At first glance, the errors didn’t provide much information, and after a search in SAP Notes, I didn’t find any directly related solutions. These vague error messages left me scratching my head, wondering what went wrong.

Troubleshooting: The Search for Answers

I started by digging into each of the error messages to see if they pointed to any common issues. After some time, it became clear that the root of the problem was related to Business System configuration. Specifically, there was no Business System entry for my S/4HANA system in the System Landscape Directory (SLD).

What is the SLD, and why is it important?

The System Landscape Directory (SLD) is an important component in SAP that holds information about the system landscape, including the system’s technical data, configuration, and communication setup. The Business System entry in the SLD is crucial for the system to recognize and connect with other systems during processes like data migration.

Without a proper Business System entry, SAP can’t identify the correct system to interact with during the migration process, leading to errors like the ones I encountered.

The Solution: Creating the Business System Entry

Once I pinpointed the issue, the fix was relatively straightforward. Here’s how I resolved the problem:

Step 1: Log in to the System Landscape Directory (SLD)

I first logged in to the SLD system through the URL. If you're unsure where your SLD is hosted, it’s typically available at a URL like:

http://<your-sld-server>:<port>/sld/

At this point, you should be able to see your system landscape.

Figure 2. Main SLD screen

Step 2: Create a Business System Entry for Your S/4HANA System

Inside the SLD, I needed to create a new Business System entry for my S/4HANA system. This entry is necessary for SAP to know where to send the data during the migration. Here’s how I did it:

  1. Navigate to the Business Systems section.
  2. Click "Create" to add a new Business System entry.
  3. Fill in the required details like the name of your system, its type (e.g., S/4HANA), and other technical details such as its ID, host, and instance number.
    (You can copy and adjust details from some other existing Business System)
  4. Save the entry. This will add your S/4HANA system as a valid Business System in the SLD.

Figure 3. Business Systems

Step 3: Re-run the Migration

After saving the Business System entry, I went back to the Migrate Your Data Fiori app and tried the migration again. This time, I clicked Migrate, and… success! The process completed without any errors.

Figure 4. Migration completed Successfully

Figure 5. No more Error messages

Why Did This Work?

The issue was that the Business System configuration was missing in the SLD, and as a result, SAP couldn’t determine where to send the data during the migration. By adding the correct Business System entry, I provided the system with the information it needed to communicate with the target system, resolving the errors I encountered.

This step is often overlooked, but it’s crucial for ensuring smooth communication between SAP systems during processes like data migration.

Additional Tips:

While this issue was specific to the Business System configuration, here are a few general tips for troubleshooting similar issues in the future:

  1. Check System Logs: Always check the system logs and trace files for more detailed error messages that might provide more insights.
  2. SAP Notes and Community Forums: If you can't find a solution in SAP Notes, check the SAP Community forums. Other users may have encountered similar problems and found solutions.
  3. Double-check your XML Template: Sometimes the issue might be with the data in your XML template. Make sure that all required fields are filled out correctly, and the format matches the expected template.

Conclusion:

If you’re encountering the GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR error in the Migrate Your Data Fiori app, I hope this blog helps you troubleshoot the issue. The root cause for me was the missing Business System entry in the System Landscape Directory (SLD), which led to the migration failure. Once I created the entry, everything worked as expected.

Have you faced similar errors? Or perhaps you have a different solution? Feel free to share your experience in the comments below. Let’s help each other out!

Tags:

SAP, Fiori App, Migrate Your Data, GET_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_ERROR, S/4HANA, Data Migration, System Error, Business System, SLD, SAP Troubleshooting

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs
entry.content:
Product Information

Betty Guedez

April 2ND, 2025

SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs in a Nutshell

Updated as of release 2023

Let's have a look at a highly relevant topic when transitioning to SAP S/4HANA, especially now that we are in 2025: Compatibility Packs: how to understand them in an easy way, where to get information about them and what the timelines are now in which Compatibility Packs will be operating, depending on the strategy recently released by SAP.

I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I feel honored to come back to this community with an SAP strategic topic like this.

If we ask Joule, we get that "... Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA are designed to ensure that certain functionalities from older SAP systems remain available in the new SAP S/4HANA environment. These packs allow customers to continue using specific features (...), providing a bridge during the transition period. They help maintain compatibility and support business continuity while customers adapt to the new system...".

Compatibility Packs are functionalities that were deployed as embedded processes in the SAP ERP and that will remain available in the SAP S/4HANA for a specific period, at the end of which SAP will execute the final stages of the strategy.

In a recent announcement, SAP shared a new timeline for Compatibility Packs. Now, together with Customer Service (CS), Logistics Execution and Transportation (LE-TRA) and Production Planning - Process Industries (PP-PI) (specific solutions that already had their expiration period set for 2030), all RISE with SAP agreements can continue using their Compatibility Packs until 2030 (not 2025 anymore). This is carefully explained in SAP Note 2269324 - Compatibility Scope Matrix for SAP S/4HANA

Figure 1: SAP S/4HANA Compatibility Packs timeline as of January 2025

Compatibility Packs, Readiness Check and Simplification Item Check

As mentioned above, SAP's Compatibility Packs offer customers temporary and limited usage rights to run specific SAP ERP solutions they have licensed on SAP S/4HANA, as outlined in their agreements. It is crucial to know this at the time of thinking about transitioning to SAP S/4HANA. That is why the SAP Readiness Check shows a Compatibility Scope Analysis on its main dashboard, providing information about simplification items related to Compatibility Packs and their status.

Figure 2: SAP Readiness Check Dashboard

In the SAP Readiness Check dashboard, 2 categories under Compatibility Scope are visible: Relevance and Status. By Relevance: each item in the list is related to specific compatibility package ID and some other details like Description, Component and the specific business impact note that must be carefully reviewed. If there is an alternative solution, this detail is also provided.

By selecting Compatibility Scope by Status, you will see information on different alternatives (if available), along with the previously mentioned details.

Figure 3: SAP Readiness Check Compatibility Scope Analysis

Later, during the transition to SAP S/4HANA process, Compatibility Scope details are provided in the SAP Simplification Item Check (SIC), issued in the SAP ERP backend. The procedure to find them is as follows:

Through transactions SA38 or SE38, execute the program /SDF/RC_START_CHECK, and use theRelevance Check to identify what functionalities are currently in use.

Figure 4: SAP Simplification Item Check

Then, analyzing the content of the column labeled "Category", filtering it to include only "Non-strategic" values (according to SAP Note 2931193 - Explanation of Simplification Item Categories) - which is easier after downloading it to an Excel spreadsheet:

Figure 5: Compatibility Packs Analysis after downloaded from SIC

For both cases, SAP Readiness Check and SAP Simplification Item Check, the second analysis for these objects must take place:

  1. Non-strategic Function (alternative exists): Another solution for the functionality exists to fulfill all major requirements expected. Transition must be evaluated and planned. Example: Simplification Item SI13: FIN_CO S4TWL - EC-PCA - Classical Profit Center Accounting Planning, can be replaced by SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning with SAP S/4HANA (SAC), according to SAP Note 2993220 - S4TWL - EC-PCA - Classical Profit Center Accounting Planning
  2. Non-strategic Function (alternative exists with roadmap): An alternative functionality is available in SAP S/4HANA, although the offering varies in scope. Those variations have been included in a roadmap for that alternative. Example: SI8_FIN_General S4TWL - Report Writer / Report Painter in Finance and Controlling, explained in SAP Note 2997574 S4TWL - Report Writer / Report Painter in Finance and Controlling
  3. Non-strategic Function (alternative planned): The successor functionality is not yet available, but there is a roadmap. Example: Simplification Item SI01: CS - S4TWL - Customer Service, SAP Note 2962632 - S4TWL - Customer Service.
  4. Non-strategic Function (no alternative planned): For these, there is currently no replacement planned. One example of this is SI7_FIN_AA - S4TWL - Group Assets. SAP Note 3532580 - Group Assets: Succession solutions contains guidance on how to proceed with this.

Compatibility Packs in the SAP Early Watch Report

As many of you probably know, the SAP Early Watch (aka SAP EWA) is an automatic service at no extra charge, that analyzes the areas of an SAP running instance, providing information on situations (maybe critical or not) and proposing solutions to protect performance and stability of the system, usually issued on a weekly basis. It is an SAP service that has been among us for long time now. For setup steps of this diagnostic, please refer to SAP Note 2520319 - How to access the SAP EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) apps in SAP for Me.

The SAP EWA provides various diagnostics and statistics, like risks that can affect business continuity, critical KPIs per system, bottlenecks, general security alerts and recommendations to improve system performance and operations. SAP EWA also brings risk mitigation strategies and a long list of insights. Additionally, the SAP EWA includes information on the usage of Simplification Items related to Compatibility Packs that are approaching an expiration date in an SAP S/4HANA system.

Written or online, the SAP EWA delivers information about the status of Compatibility Packs in an SAP system. In SAP For Me, once you get to the SAP EWA Solution Finder, after opening the report chapter for the system, a list of the Compatibility Packs in use over the last three months is pulled. For each, a specific SAP Note with background information of the business process and an alternative path forward is provided.

So, if already in an SAP S/4HANA system, the SAP EWA helps track the progress in transitioning to the new solutions when still using Compatibility Packs. With the SAP EWA, following the usage of each Compatibility Item and tracing the progress in replacing the functionality is an ongoing process.

For RISE with SAP customers, the SAP EWA tracks the replacement of Compatibility Packs with the expiration set for 2030, so no messages will be raised for now. For others, the SAP EWA highlights Compatibility Packs expiring at the end of 2025 in red, as a warning that actions must be taken soon.

As a conclusion, I must say that making a decision about what to do next, in terms of transitioning from the old to the new redesigned, improved business processes is something that shall not wait too long. Compatibility Packs bring a unique opportunity to adopt the new innovations available, gain efficiencies by leveraging new and better practices and give business users the time to adapt to them with minimal disruption and in a timely manner.

Get the best of it!

I hope you find reading this blog worthwhile.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.
